message:
Replace regex glob matcher with O(n) byte algorithm, harden WAL recovery, expand runtime tests

Core improvements across frankenterm-core:

Glob matching (events.rs):
  - Replace regex-based match_rule_glob with an iterative byte-level
    two-pointer algorithm supporting * and ? wildcards in O(n) time
  - Eliminates dependency on fancy_regex and thread-local LRU regex cache
  - Simpler, faster, allocation-free pattern matching for event rules

WAL recovery (wal_engine.rs):
  - Fix DiskWal::load to detect and truncate dangling tail bytes that
    occur when actual file size exceeds the last good entry offset, even
    when no corrupt entries are found (e.g. partial length prefix writes)
  - Fix test code: use .unwrap() on append() and checkpoint() returns
    instead of silently ignoring errors

Policy tests (policy.rs):
  - Expand injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface test to verify
    PolicySurface::Mux assertion for all four ActorKind variants
    (Human, Robot, Mcp, Workflow) instead of just Robot

Runtime compatibility tests (runtime_compat_smoke.rs):
  - Replace flaky sleep-based task detection with channel-based
    synchronization for deterministic detached task verification
  - Add runtime_helpers_support_mpsc_round_trip: validates mpsc_send
    and mpsc_recv_option helper round-trip on current_thread runtime
  - Add runtime_helpers_support_watch_change_consumption: validates
    watch channel send/changed/borrow_and_update_clone lifecycle
  - Add timeout_reports_elapsed_for_slow_future: verifies timeout()
    returns elapsed error for futures exceeding the deadline

Infrastructure:
  - Cargo.lock: dependency version updates
  - .beads/issues.jsonl: issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
